description_long: This radiator features a rectangular, vertically oriented body with a series of three prominent, curved, horizontal fin assemblies mounted on its front face. The fins are composed of multiple parallel, dark metallic slats, spaced evenly to allow airflow and maximize heat dissipation. The main body appears to be a solid, light-colored panel, likely made of metal, providing structural support and housing for the heating elements. The radiator is designed to be mounted flush against a wall, with the fin assemblies protruding outward, creating a visually striking and functional design. No visible valves or pipe connections are present on the exterior, suggesting they may be located on the rear or concealed edges. The overall aesthetic is modern and utilitarian, suitable for urban interiors or public spaces.
description: A modern, wall-mounted radiator with three curved, horizontal fin assemblies on a solid rectangular panel. The design features dark metallic slats for efficient heat dissipation and a clean, contemporary look.
description_view_0: Front view showing three curved, horizontal fin assemblies with parallel slats mounted on a light rectangular panel.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the protrusion of the fin assemblies from the flat panel, emphasizing their depth and curvature.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, solid panel with no visible fins or features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the depth and structure of the fin assemblies.
attribute_fin_arrangement: horizontal curved slats in three stacked assemblies
